title.zh: 卷五十七下 司馬相如傳
title.raw: 卷五十七下 司馬相如傳 Volume 57b: Sima Xiangru

sentences.zh: 司馬相如（下）

translations.literal: Sima Xiangru (Part Two).
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru (Part Two).

sentences.zh: 相如為郎數歲，會唐蒙使略通夜郎、僰中，發巴、蜀吏卒，千人，郡又多為發轉漕萬餘人，用軍興法誅其渠率。

translations.literal: Xiangru had been a gentleman attendant for several years when, as it happened, Tang Meng was sent to open communications with Yelang and Bozhong; he mobilized officers and soldiers of Ba and Shu, a thousand men, and the commanderies further raised for him over ten thousand men for transport and supply; he used the laws governing military emergencies to execute their ringleaders.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru had served as a gentleman attendant for several years when Tang Meng was dispatched to push the frontier toward Yelang and the Bozhong region. Tang called up a thousand officers and troops from Ba and Shu, and the commanderies raised more than ten thousand additional laborers for transport and supply. Invoking the statutes used in wartime mobilization, he put their leaders to death.

sentences.zh: 巴、蜀民大驚恐。

translations.literal: The people of Ba and Shu were greatly alarmed and terrified.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Ba and Shu were thrown into panic.

sentences.zh: 上聞之，乃遣相如責唐蒙等，因諭告巴、蜀民以非上意。

translations.literal: When the emperor heard of it, he then sent Xiangru to rebuke Tang Meng and the others, and thereby instructed and informed the people of Ba and Shu that this was not the emperor's intention.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor learned of the unrest, he sent Xiangru to reprimand Tang Meng and his associates and to make clear to the people of Ba and Shu that none of this had been the sovereign's will.

sentences.zh: 檄曰：

translations.literal: The proclamation said:
translations.idiomatic: The proclamation read:

sentences.zh: 夫邊郡之士，聞烽舉燧燔，皆攝弓而弛，荷兵而走，流汗相屬，惟恐居後，觸白刃，冒流矢，議不反顧，計不旋踵，人懷怒心，如報私仇。

translations.literal: The men of the border commanderies, hearing beacons raised and signal fires lit, all seize their bows with strings slackened, shoulder their weapons and run, sweat streaming one after another, fearing only that they will lag behind; they meet white blades, brave flying arrows, deliberate without turning their heads, plan without pivoting on their heels; each man harbors a heart of rage, as if avenging a private feud.
translations.idiomatic: When the beacon fires go up along the frontier, the garrison men snatch up their bows, shoulder their arms, and run until they are drenched in sweat, each terrified of falling behind. They throw themselves against naked steel and whistling arrows without a thought for retreat, burning to strike as if settling a blood feud of their own.

sentences.zh: 彼豈樂死惡生，非編列之民，而與巴、蜀異主哉？

translations.literal: How could they delight in death and hate life? Are they not registered commoners under the same rulers as Ba and Shu?
translations.idiomatic: Surely they do not love death and despise life; they are ordinary subjects under the same throne as the people of Ba and Shu.

sentences.zh: 計深慮遠，急國家之難，而樂盡人臣之道也。

translations.literal: They plan deeply and think far, hasten to the state's crisis, and delight in fulfilling the way of a minister.
translations.idiomatic: They think ahead, rush to the nation's hour of need, and take honor in doing everything a loyal minister ought to do.

sentences.zh: 故有剖符之封，析圭而爵，位為通侯，居列東第。

translations.literal: Therefore there are enfeoffments with tallies split in two, scepter-jade divided for rank, position as full marquis, and residence in the mansions east of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: That is why they receive investiture with split tallies, titles tied to ritual jade, rank as full marquises, and mansions in the noble quarter east of the palace.

sentences.zh: 終則遺顯號於後世，傳土地於子孫，事行甚忠敬，居位甚安佚，名聲施於無窮，功烈著而不滅。

translations.literal: In the end they bequeath illustrious titles to later generations, transmit land to sons and grandsons; their conduct is most loyal and reverent, their tenure most secure and easeful; their fame spreads without end, their merit abides undying.
translations.idiomatic: In the end they leave a glorious name to posterity and pass estates down to their children; their service is marked by deep loyalty, their posts by security and ease; their reputation never fades and their achievements are not forgotten.

sentences.zh: 是以賢人君子，肝腦塗中原，膏液潤野草而不辭也。

translations.literal: Therefore worthy men and gentlemen spread their livers and brains upon the central plain, their fat and marrow moisten the wild grass, yet they do not decline.
translations.idiomatic: That is why the best men gladly give their lives on the battlefield and let their blood soak the weeds without a second thought.

sentences.zh: 今奉幣役至南夷，即自賊殺，或亡逃抵誅，身死無名，謚為至愚，恥及父母，為天下笑。

translations.literal: Now, bearing tribute and labor service, you reach the southern barbarians, then immediately kill yourselves, or flee and face execution; you die without a good name, are posthumously styled supremely foolish, bring shame upon your parents, and are laughed at by the world.
translations.idiomatic: Yet you are sent south on imperial business with tribute and labor in train, and you turn to suicide or bolt and end on the executioner's block. You die disgraced, earn the epithet of utter folly, humiliate your parents, and become a laughingstock to the empire.

sentences.zh: 人之度量相越，豈不遠哉！

translations.literal: The gulf between men's measures—how could it not be vast!
translations.idiomatic: How vast is the distance between such conduct and theirs!

sentences.zh: 然此非獨行者之罪也，父兄之教不先，子弟之率不謹，寡廉鮮恥，而俗不長厚也。

translations.literal: Yet this is not solely the fault of those who act thus: fathers and elder brothers did not instruct them first, sons and younger brothers were not led with care; they are scant in modesty and shame, and custom does not nurture honesty.
translations.idiomatic: Nor is the blame theirs alone: elders failed to teach them early, juniors were not kept under firm guidance; shame and decency thinned out until the custom no longer bred integrity.

sentences.zh: 其被刑戮，不亦宜乎！

translations.literal: That they suffer execution and mutilation—is it not fitting!
translations.idiomatic: When such people are put to the sword, they have only themselves to blame.

sentences.zh: 陛下患使者有司之若彼，悼不肖愚民之如此，故遣信使，曉諭百姓以發卒之事，因數之以不忠死亡之罪，讓三老孝弟以不教誨之過。

translations.literal: Your Majesty grieves that envoys and officials were like that, mourns that unworthy foolish commoners were thus, and therefore sends a trusted envoy to enlighten the hundred surnames concerning the matter of raising troops, and thereby enumerates to them the crime of disloyalty leading to death, and reproaches the village elders for the fault of not teaching and instructing.
translations.idiomatic: The throne deplores officials who behaved as they did and pities ignorant folk who reacted so rashly. Hence a reliable envoy has been sent to explain why levies were ordered, to spell out how disloyalty leads to death, and to call village elders to account for failing to teach their people.

sentences.zh: 方今田時，重煩百姓，已親見近縣，恐遠所溪谷山澤之民不遍聞，檄到，亟下縣道，鹹諭陛下意，毋忽！

translations.literal: Now it is plowing season; we heavily trouble the hundred surnames; already we have personally visited the nearby counties; we fear those in distant streams, valleys, mountains, and marshes have not all heard; when the proclamation arrives, urgently transmit it down to counties and circuits, thoroughly make known the emperor's intent; do not neglect it!
translations.idiomatic: It is planting season, and the court regrets the burden on the people. Envoys have already toured the nearer counties, but those in remote valleys and hills may not have heard. When this edict reaches you, relay it at once along every county and post road so that every household understands the emperor's purpose. Do not treat it lightly.

sentences.zh: 相如還報。

translations.literal: Xiangru returned and reported.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru returned and made his report.

sentences.zh: 唐蒙已略通夜郎，因通西南夷道，發巴、蜀、廣漢卒，作者數萬人。

translations.literal: Tang Meng had already opened communications with Yelang, and on that account opened the road to the southwestern barbarians, mobilizing workers from Ba, Shu, and Guanghan, several tens of thousands of laborers.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Meng had already pushed through to Yelang and was extending the southwest road, drafting tens of thousands of laborers from Ba, Shu, and Guanghan.

sentences.zh: 治道二歲，道不成，士卒多物故，費以億萬計。

translations.literal: For two years they worked on the road; the road was not completed; many soldiers died of hardship; the expense was reckoned in the hundreds of millions.
translations.idiomatic: Work dragged on for two years without finishing the route. Casualties mounted among the troops, and costs ran into the hundreds of millions.

sentences.zh: 蜀民及漢用事者多言其不便。

translations.literal: The people of Shu and Han officials in power often said it was not advantageous.
translations.idiomatic: Shu locals and influential men at court repeatedly argued that the project was a waste.

sentences.zh: 是時邛、莋之君長聞南夷與漢通，得賞賜多，多欲願為內臣妾，請吏，比南夷。

translations.literal: At this time the lords of Qiong and Zuo heard that the southern barbarians had opened relations with Han and received rich rewards; many wished to become inner subjects and concubines-in-submission, requested officials, and asked to be treated like the southern barbarians.
translations.idiomatic: The chiefs of Qiong and Zuo learned that the southern tribes had submitted to Han and been showered with gifts; most of them now wanted to enter the register as tributaries, asked for imperial magistrates, and sought the same status as the southern peoples.

sentences.zh: 上問相如，相如曰：「邛、莋、冉、□者近署，道易通，異時嘗通為郡縣矣，至漢興而罷。

translations.literal: The emperor questioned Xiangru, and Xiangru said: "Qiong, Zuo, Ran, and Mang lie near the metropolitan area; the road is easy to open; in former times they were once connected as commanderies and counties, but at the rise of Han that was discontinued.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked Xiangru for his view. He replied: "The tribes of Qiong, Zuo, Ran, and Mang border the interior; their routes are easier than Yelang's. They were once organized as commanderies in earlier dynasties and only lapsed when the Han arose.

sentences.zh: 今誠復通，為置縣，愈於南夷。」

translations.literal: Now if we truly reopen communications, establish counties for them, it will be better than dealing with the southern barbarians.
translations.idiomatic: If we reopen ties and place magistrates among them, we will gain more for less trouble than we have had in the far south.

sentences.zh: 上以為然，乃拜相如為中郎將，建節往使。

translations.literal: The emperor deemed it right, and therefore appointed Xiangru general of the gentlemen, bearing the imperial insignia to go as envoy.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed and appointed Xiangru a general of the gentlemen, giving him the credenza and dispatching him as chief envoy.

sentences.zh: 副使者王然於、壺棄國、呂越人，馳四乘之傳，因巴、蜀吏幣物以賂西南夷。

translations.literal: Associate envoys Wang Ranyu, Hu Qiguo, and Lü Yueren galloped post relays of four horses, and by means of Ba and Shu officials presented goods to bribe the southwestern barbarians.
translations.idiomatic: His deputies Wang Ranyu, Hu Qiguo, and Lü Yueren raced ahead on four-horse relay carriages, while local officials from Ba and Shu carried gifts to win over the southwestern tribes.

sentences.zh: 至蜀，太守以下郊迎，縣令負弩矢先驅，蜀人以為寵。

translations.literal: When they reached Shu, the governor and all below came out to the suburbs to welcome them; the county magistrate bore crossbows and arrows as vanguard; the people of Shu considered it an honor.
translations.idiomatic: At their arrival in Shu the governor led his staff in a suburban welcome; the county magistrate marched ahead shouldering bow and bolts. The Shu people took it as a mark of singular favor.

sentences.zh: 於是卓王孫、臨邛諸公皆因門下獻牛、酒以交歡。

translations.literal: Thereupon Zhuo Wangsun and the gentlemen of Linqiong all by way of his retainers presented oxen and wine to cultivate good relations.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun and the leading families of Linqiong then sent cattle and wine through Xiangru's attendants to join the celebration.

sentences.zh: 卓王孫喟然而漢，自以得使女尚司馬長卿晚，乃厚分與其女財，與男等。

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun sighed with regret, thinking himself late in obtaining a son-in-law like Sima Changqing for his daughter, and therefore divided his daughter's share of wealth generously, equal to that of his sons.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun sighed that he had waited too long to gain Sima Changqing as a son-in-law; he now settled a lavish marriage portion on his daughter, equal to what he gave his sons.

sentences.zh: 相如使略定西南夷，邛、莋、再、駹、斯榆之君皆請為臣妾，除邊關，邊關益斥，西至沫、若水，南至牁牂為徼，通靈山道，橋孫水，以通邛、莋。

translations.literal: Xiangru as envoy broadly pacified the southwestern barbarians; the lords of Qiong, Zuo, Zai, Mang, and Siyu all requested to become subjects and concubines-in-submission; border passes were removed; the border passes were further extended west to Mo and Ruoshui, south to Zangke as the outer cordon; the Ling Mountain road was opened, a bridge thrown across the Sun River, to communicate with Qiong and Zuo.
translations.idiomatic: On mission Xiangru brought the southwest under control: the rulers of Qiong, Zuo, Zai, Mang, and Siyu all asked to submit as tributaries. Frontier barriers came down and the line of outposts stretched west to the Mo and Ruoshui rivers and south to Zangke as the outer perimeter. Roads pierced Mount Ling, bridges spanned the Sun River, and traffic linked Qiong and Zuo.

sentences.zh: 還報，天子大說。

translations.literal: He returned and reported; the Son of Heaven was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: When he reported back, the emperor was delighted.

sentences.zh: 相如使時，蜀長老多言通西南夷之不為用，大臣亦以為然。

translations.literal: When Xiangru had been on mission, Shu elders often said opening communications with the southwestern barbarians was useless; great ministers also thought it so.
translations.idiomatic: While Xiangru was away, Shu notables kept saying that the southwest road served no purpose, and senior ministers at court agreed.

sentences.zh: 相如欲諫，業已建之，不敢，乃著書，借蜀父老為辭，而己詰難之，以風天子，且因宣其使指，令百姓皆知天子意。

translations.literal: Xiangru wished to remonstrate, but since he had already initiated the project, he did not dare; he therefore composed a book, borrowing the words of Shu elders as his text, while he himself posed the hard questions, in order to influence the Son of Heaven, and also thereby proclaim the intent of his mission, so that the hundred surnames would all know the emperor's mind.
translations.idiomatic: He wanted to argue the case but dared not, having himself sponsored the scheme. Instead he drafted a dialogue, putting the objections in the mouths of Shu elders and answering them in his own voice, both to sway the throne and to spell out what his mission had meant so that every household would grasp the emperor's purpose.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: The piece begins:

sentences.zh: 漢興七十有八載，德茂存乎六世，威武紛雲，港恩汪濊，群生霑濡，洋溢乎方外。

translations.literal: The Han has risen seventy-eight years; virtue flourishes through six reigns; martial awesomeness piles like clouds; imperial favor spreads vast and deep; the myriad creatures are soaked and drenched; it overflows beyond the regions.
translations.idiomatic: Seventy-eight years have passed since the Han rose; through six sovereigns virtue has deepened, military glory towers like clouds, and imperial kindness spreads wide until every living thing shares in it and the influence reaches beyond the borders.

sentences.zh: 於是乃命使西征，隨流而攘，風之所被，罔不披靡。

translations.literal: Thereupon he ordered envoys to march west, following the currents and pushing outward; where the wind blew, none did not bend low.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore sent armies west along the rivers; wherever their influence reached, resistance collapsed.

sentences.zh: 因朝冉從□，定莋存邛，略斯榆，舉苞蒲，結軌還轅，東鄉將報，至於蜀都。

translations.literal: Thereby he received court from Ran, followed Mang, stabilized Zuo, preserved Qiong, overran Siyu, raised Bao and Pu, hitched the traces and turned the hubs, faced east about to report, and arrived at the Shu capital.
translations.idiomatic: Ran came to court, Mang followed in train, Zuo was secured, Qiong preserved, Siyu overrun, Bao and Pu brought to heel; the expedition wheeled its chariots homeward, turned east toward the capital for its report, and halted at Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 耆老大夫搢紳先生之徒二十有七人，儼然造焉。

translations.literal: Twenty-seven men—elders, grandees, girdle-and-ribbon gentlemen—solemnly came forward.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty-seven elders, officials, and gentry in full dress presented themselves with grave formality.

sentences.zh: 辭畢，進曰：「蓋聞天子之於夷狄也，其義羈縻勿絕而已。

translations.literal: When the words were finished, they advanced and said: "We have heard that the Son of Heaven's treatment of the Yi and Di is, in principle, to hold them on loose reins without severing ties.
translations.idiomatic: When he had finished speaking, they stepped forward: "We have always understood that the Son of Heaven's policy toward barbarians is to keep them on a loose tether without cutting them off.

sentences.zh: 今罷三郡之士，通夜郎之塗，三年於茲，而功不竟。

translations.literal: Now you have dismissed the levies of three commanderies, opened the road to Yelang; for three years until now, yet the work is not completed.
translations.idiomatic: Yet you have drafted three commanderies, opened the road to Yelang, and three years on the work is still unfinished.

sentences.zh: 士卒勞倦，萬民不贍；

translations.literal: Soldiers are weary; the myriad people lack sufficiency;
translations.idiomatic: The troops are exhausted and the people can barely feed themselves;

sentences.zh: 今又接之以西夷，百姓力屈，恐不能卒業，此亦使者這累也，竊為左右患之。

translations.literal: now you further join them to the western barbarians; the strength of the hundred surnames is bent; we fear they cannot finish the undertaking; this is also the fault of the envoys; we privately worry for those on your left and right.
translations.idiomatic: now you would yoke them to the tribes of the west. Popular strength is spent; we doubt the task can ever be completed. That would be the envoys' legacy as well, and we tremble for your advisors.

sentences.zh: 且夫邛、莋、西僰之與中國並也，歷年茲多，不可記已。

translations.literal: Moreover Qiong, Zuo, and the western Bo have existed alongside the Central States for many years; the years are so many they cannot be recorded.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, Qiong, Zuo, and the western Bo have stood beside China for ages beyond counting.

sentences.zh: 仁者不以德來，強者不以力並，意者殆不可乎！

translations.literal: The humane do not come by virtue; the strong do not annex by force; in our opinion perhaps it cannot be done!
translations.idiomatic: The humane ruler does not win them by kindness alone; the strong state does not swallow them by force alone—and we suspect neither course will really work!

sentences.zh: 今割齊民以附夷狄，弊所恃以事無用，鄙人固陋，不識所謂。」

translations.literal: Now you carve out orderly commoners to attach to the Yi and Di, exhaust what you rely on to serve the useless; we petty men are indeed rustic and do not understand what is meant.
translations.idiomatic: To strip our settled farmers for the sake of barbarians, to waste what we depend on to serve a useless venture—we are plain folk, perhaps, but we cannot see the sense of it.

sentences.zh: 使者曰：「烏謂此乎？

translations.literal: The envoy said: "How can you say this?
translations.idiomatic: The envoy replied: "How can you speak so?

sentences.zh: 必若所云，則是蜀不變服而巴不化俗也，僕尚惡聞若說。

translations.literal: If it must be as you say, then Shu would not change its dress and Ba would not transform its customs; I would still loathe to hear such talk.
translations.idiomatic: If your words were true, Shu would never have adopted Han costume nor Ba Han manners—and I would still shrink from such talk.

sentences.zh: 然斯事體大，固非觀者之所覯也。

translations.literal: Yet this affair is great in scope; it is indeed not what onlookers have witnessed.
translations.idiomatic: The matter is too large for casual observers to judge.

sentences.zh: 余之行急，其詳不可得聞已。

translations.literal: My journey was hurried; the details could not be heard in full.
translations.idiomatic: My mission left no time; I could not hear you out in every particular.

sentences.zh: 請為大夫粗陳其略：

translations.literal: Allow me to outline the rough points for you gentlemen:
translations.idiomatic: Let me sketch the main lines for you:

sentences.zh: 「蓋世必有非常之人，然後有非常之事；

translations.literal: "In the age there must be extraordinary men, and then there are extraordinary affairs;
translations.idiomatic: "Extraordinary times call for extraordinary men;

sentences.zh: 有非常之事，然後有非常之功。

translations.literal: there are extraordinary affairs, and then there are extraordinary achievements.
translations.idiomatic: Extraordinary enterprises yield extraordinary achievements.

sentences.zh: 非常者，固常人之所異也。

translations.literal: What is extraordinary is indeed what common men find strange.
translations.idiomatic: What common people call strange is simply what they have never seen before.

sentences.zh: 故曰非常之元，黎民懼焉；

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: when extraordinary beginnings arise, the black-haired people fear them;
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: at the first stirrings of a great venture the people are afraid;

sentences.zh: 及臻厥成，天下晏如也。」

translations.literal: when it reaches completion, the realm is tranquil.
translations.idiomatic: when it succeeds, the realm rests easy.

sentences.zh: 「昔者，洪水沸出，氾濫衍溢，民人升降移徙，崎嶇而不安。

translations.literal: "In antiquity, flood waters boiled forth, overflowed and spread; the people ascended and descended, moved and shifted, perilous and unsettled.
translations.idiomatic: "In ancient times the flood surged until it drowned the land; people climbed hills and fled from place to place, never finding peace.

sentences.zh: 夏後氏戚之，乃堙洪原，決江疏河，灑沈澹災，東歸之於海，而天下永寧。

translations.literal: The house of Xia grieved over it; they then dammed the flood source, cut through the Yangtze and dredged the Yellow River, scattered the submerged and drained the disaster, returning it east to the sea, and the realm was forever tranquil.
translations.idiomatic: The Xia founders mourned that suffering: they dammed the headwaters, cut channels through the Yangtze and Yellow rivers, drained the drowned lowlands, and sent the waters east to the sea until the world knew lasting calm.

sentences.zh: 當斯之勤，豈惟民哉？

translations.literal: In that labor, was it only the people?
translations.idiomatic: Do you imagine the burden fell on the common people alone?

sentences.zh: 心煩於慮，而身親其勞，躬傶骿胝無□，膚不生毛，故休烈顯乎無窮，聲稱浹乎於茲。」

translations.literal: The mind was vexed in planning, yet the body personally undertook the toil; he bent his waist and thighs until his soles were calloused without [rest]; skin grew no hair; therefore splendid merit shines without end, fame spreads to this day.
translations.idiomatic: The ruler's mind labored over every plan while his body did the heaviest work—his shins and soles hardened with callus, his skin bare of down—so that his glory still lights the ages and his name rings down to us.

sentences.zh: 「且夫賢君之踐位也，豈特委瑣握齪，拘文牽俗，循誦習傳，當世取說云爾哉！

translations.literal: "Moreover, when a worthy lord mounts the throne, how could it be only petty, cramped, bound by precedent and tied by custom, reciting transmitted learning, pleasing the age—that is all?
translations.idiomatic: "And when a true king takes the throne, does he mean to stay petty and cramped, shackled by precedent and slave to gossip, merely repeating old formulas to win easy applause?

sentences.zh: 必將崇論□議，創業垂統，為萬世規。

translations.literal: He must elevate discourse and broaden counsel, found enterprises and hand down a continuing line, setting a pattern for ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: No—he must voice great policies and bold counsel, found institutions that will last, and lay down models for endless generations.

sentences.zh: 故馳騖乎兼容並包，而勤思乎參天貳地。

translations.literal: Therefore he gallops in inclusive breadth, and diligently thinks of matching heaven and doubling earth.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore rides forth on a course broad enough to embrace all under heaven and sets his sights as high as the sky and as deep as the earth.

sentences.zh: 且《詩》不雲乎：『普天之下，莫非王土；

translations.literal: Moreover does not the Classic of Songs say: "Under the whole heaven, none is not the king's land;
translations.idiomatic: Does not the Odes say, "Under the whole sky every scrap of soil belongs to the king;

sentences.zh: 率土之濱，莫非王臣。』

translations.literal: along the banks of the land, none is not the king's subject.
translations.idiomatic: along every shore of the realm every man is the king's subject.

sentences.zh: 是以六合之內，八方之外，浸淫衍溢，懷生之物有不浸潤於澤者，賢君恥之。

translations.literal: Therefore within the six coordinates, beyond the eight directions, soaking and overflowing, if any living creature does not receive the moisture of favor, the worthy lord is ashamed of it.
translations.idiomatic: Within the six directions and beyond the eight outer regions, if any creature fails to feel the royal bounty, a worthy ruler counts it his shame.

sentences.zh: 今封疆之內，冠帶之倫，鹹獲嘉祉，靡有闕遺矣。

translations.literal: Now within the sealed frontiers, among the capped-and-girded classes, all have received fine blessing; none is left lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Inside the frontiers every belted official already shares in the blessing; none is left out.

sentences.zh: 而夷狄殊俗之國，遼絕異黨之域，舟車不通，人跡罕至，政教未加，流風猶微，內之則犯義侵禮於邊境，外之則邪行橫作，放殺其上，君臣易位，尊卑失序，父兄不辜，幼孤為奴虜，係累號泣。

translations.literal: But the countries of the Yi and Di with alien customs, regions of remote separation and different clans, where boats and carts do not reach and human tracks are seldom seen, to which government and instruction have not yet been extended and drifting influence is still slight—if brought within, they violate duty and encroach on ritual at the borders; if left without, wicked deeds run rampant, they slay their superiors, lord and minister exchange places, noble and base lose order, fathers and elder brothers suffer unjust death, infants and orphans become slaves and captives, tied and led off, wailing and weeping.
translations.idiomatic: Yet beyond lie tribes of strange dress and alien speech, lands cut off by distance where neither cart nor boat can pass and few travelers go, places never touched by law or rite. Leave them outside and they raid the frontier; fold them in and murder and revolt multiply—subjects kill their lords, high and low are reversed, the innocent are cut down, children are dragged off as slaves, roped together and sobbing.

sentences.zh: 內鄉而怨，曰：『蓋聞中國有至仁焉，德洋恩普，物磨不得其所，今獨曷為遺己！』

translations.literal: Inwardly they incline and resent, saying: "We have heard that in the Central States there is utmost benevolence; virtue spreads wide and favor is universal; no creature fails to find its place—why alone are we left out!
translations.idiomatic: They turn their faces toward China and cry, "We hear that the Middle Kingdom is ruled by perfect humanity, that virtue floods every corner until nothing lacks its place—why then are we alone cast off?

sentences.zh: 舉踵思慕，若枯旱之望雨，□夫為之垂涕，況乎上聖，又烏能已？

translations.literal: They stand on tiptoe and long for it, like drought looking for rain; [even] the husbandman sheds tears for them—how much more the supreme sage—how could he stop?
translations.idiomatic: They strain on tiptoe toward us like parched fields waiting for rain; the plowman weeps for them—how could the sage on high hold back?

sentences.zh: 故北出師以討強胡，南馳使以誚勁越。

translations.literal: Therefore he marched troops north to chastise the mighty Hu, galloped envoys south to reprove the fierce Yue.
translations.idiomatic: That is why armies went north against the Xiongnu and envoys raced south to rebuke the Yue.

sentences.zh: 四面風德，二方之君鱗集仰流，願得受號者以億計。

translations.literal: On four sides they turn to virtue; the lords of two regions swarm like fish swimming upstream, those wishing to receive titles numbering in the hundreds of millions.
translations.idiomatic: On every side peoples incline toward our virtue; chiefs of two quarters swarm upstream like spawning fish, and those begging for investiture are counted by the hundred thousand.

sentences.zh: 故乃關沫、若，徼牂牁，鏤靈山，梁孫原，創道德之塗，垂仁義之統，將博恩廣施，遠撫長駕，使疏逖不閉，昒爽暗昧得耀乎光明，以偃甲兵於此，而息討伐於彼。

translations.literal: Therefore he closed Mo and Ruo, cordoned Zangke, carved Ling Mountain, bridged the Sun plain, opened the path of virtue and humanity, hung the cord of benevolence and right, intending broadly to extend favor and widely to soothe and long to drive, so that the remote are not shut off, the dim and dark may shine in the bright light, thereby to lay down arms here and cease punitive expeditions there.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the passes on the Mo and Ruo were shut, Zangke was ringed with outposts, Mount Ling was cut through, the Sun River bridged, opening a highway of moral sway and stretching the net of benevolence and duty—intending to spread grace far and wide, to bind the distant in peace, to leave no far corner closed, to bring twilight peoples into the light, to stack weapons here and end campaigns there.

sentences.zh: 遐邇一體，中外禔福，不亦康乎？

translations.literal: Far and near are one body, within and without share blessing—is it not peaceable?
translations.idiomatic: Near and far become one fabric, inner and outer alike secure—is that not the true peace?

sentences.zh: 夫拯民於沈溺，奉至尊之休德，反衰世之陵夷，繼周氏之絕業，天子之急務也。

translations.literal: To rescue the people from drowning, to uphold the supreme one's splendid virtue, to reverse the decline of a fading age, to continue the broken enterprise of the house of Zhou—these are the Son of Heaven's urgent tasks.
translations.idiomatic: Snatching people from the flood, displaying the sovereign's perfect virtue, reversing the rot of a declining age, and taking up the broken work of the Zhou—these are the throne's pressing duties.

sentences.zh: 百姓雖勞，又惡可以已哉？

translations.literal: Though the hundred surnames are weary, how can it be stopped?
translations.idiomatic: Even if the people grow tired, how can such work simply stop?

sentences.zh: 「且夫王者固未有不始於憂勤，而終於佚樂者也。

translations.literal: "Moreover the kingly man indeed never fails to begin in anxious toil and end in ease and pleasure.
translations.idiomatic: No true king has ever begun in comfort and ended in idleness; he always starts in care and finishes in repose.

sentences.zh: 然則受命之符合在於此。

translations.literal: Then the tokens of received mandate fit in this.
translations.idiomatic: That is where the mandate of heaven shows itself.

sentences.zh: 方將增太山之封，加梁父之事，鳴和鸞，揚樂頒，上鹹五，下登三。

translations.literal: He is about to add the feng sacrifice on Mount Tai, join the shan on Liangfu, ring the harmonious bells, raise the music of praise, match the Five above, rival the Three below.
translations.idiomatic: He will soon add the great feng on Mount Tai and the shan on Liangfu, ring the ritual bells, raise the hymns of praise, rival the Five Thearchs above and the Three August Ones below.

sentences.zh: 觀者未睹指，聽者未聞音，猶焦朋已翔乎寥廓，而羅者猶視乎藪澤，悲夫！」

translations.literal: The onlookers have not yet seen his aim, the listeners have not yet heard the tone—it is as if the giant roc already soars in the void, while the netter still stares into the marsh—alas!
translations.idiomatic: Spectators have not yet grasped his purpose, listeners have not caught the music—it is as though the great roc were already wheeling in the empyrean while the fowler squints into the reeds. How sad!

sentences.zh: 於是諸大夫茫然喪其所懷來，失厥所以進，喟然並稱曰：「允哉漢德，此鄙人之所願聞也。

translations.literal: Thereupon the various grandees blankly lost what they had brought in their minds, forgot how they had meant to advance, and with a sigh together said: "Truly it is Han virtue; this is what we petty men wished to hear.
translations.idiomatic: The elders stood dumbstruck, their prepared speeches forgotten. Sighing, they said as one: "So this is Han virtue—we have heard what we needed to hear.

sentences.zh: 百姓雖勞，請以身先之。」

translations.literal: Though the hundred surnames are weary, we request to take the lead with our bodies.
translations.idiomatic: Even if the people must toil, let us go first.

sentences.zh: 敞罔靡徙，遷延而辭避。

translations.literal: Dispirited and without moving, they delayed and declined in excuse.
translations.idiomatic: They shuffled, empty of further argument, and withdrew in confusion.

sentences.zh: 其後人有上書言相如使時受金，失官。

translations.literal: Afterward someone submitted a memorial saying Xiangru had accepted gold while on mission, and he lost his office.
translations.idiomatic: Later a memorial accused Xiangru of taking bribes on mission, and he was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，復召為郎。

translations.literal: After somewhat more than a year, he was again summoned to be a gentleman attendant.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a year later he was recalled to serve again as a gentleman attendant.

sentences.zh: 相如口吃而善著書。

translations.literal: Xiangru stammered but was good at writing books.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru stammered yet excelled at literary composition.

sentences.zh: 常有消渴病。

translations.literal: He often had the wasting-thirst illness.
translations.idiomatic: He suffered chronically from diabetes.

sentences.zh: 與卓氏婚，饒於財。

translations.literal: He married into the Zhuo clan and was rich in wealth.
translations.idiomatic: His marriage to the Zhuo family left him wealthy.

sentences.zh: 故其仕宦，未嘗肯與公卿國家之事，常稱疾閒居，不慕官爵。

translations.literal: Therefore in his official career he was never willing to join in the affairs of the dukes and ministers of state; he often pleaded illness and dwelt at leisure, not coveting rank or salary.
translations.idiomatic: Hence he seldom took part in high policy, often pleaded illness, lived quietly at home, and showed no ambition for office or title.

sentences.zh: 嘗從上至長楊獵。

translations.literal: He once accompanied the emperor to hunt at Changyang.
translations.idiomatic: He once accompanied the emperor on a hunt at the Changyang park.

sentences.zh: 是時天子方好自擊熊豕，馳逐野獸，相如因上疏諫。

translations.literal: At this time the Son of Heaven was fond of personally striking bears and boars, galloping in pursuit of wild beasts; Xiangru therefore submitted a memorial remonstrating.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had taken to killing bears and boars with his own hands and racing after game; Xiangru therefore presented a written remonstrance.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: It read:

sentences.zh: 臣聞物有同類而殊能者，故力稱烏獲，捷言慶忌，勇其賁、育。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that among things there are those of the same kind yet different capabilities; therefore in strength they cite Wuhuo, in swiftness they speak of Qingji, in courage they name Ben and Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Your servant has heard that creatures of one kind may differ in gifts: hence men praise Wuhuo for strength, Qingji for speed, Ben and Yu for valor.

sentences.zh: 臣之愚，竊以為人誠有之，獸亦宜然。

translations.literal: Your servant's foolish opinion privately holds that if it is so with men, it should also be so with beasts.
translations.idiomatic: In my ignorance I believe that if this is true of men, it is true of beasts as well.

sentences.zh: 今陛下好陵阻險，射猛獸，卒然遇逸材之獸，駭不存之地，犯屬車之清塵，輿不及還轅，人不暇施巧，雖有烏獲、逢蒙之技不能用，枯木朽株盡為難矣。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty delights in scaling steep peril and shooting fierce beasts; suddenly you may meet a beast of outstanding mettle, startled in a place where you cannot survive, disturbing the clear dust of the accompanying chariots; the carriage has no time to turn the hubs, men have no time to apply their skills; though you had the arts of Wuhuo and Peng Meng they could not be used; withered trees and rotted stumps all become hazards.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Your Majesty loves to climb dangerous ground and shoot savage game. Should some powerful beast bolt from cover in a spot where the chariot cannot save you, the dust of your escort would be thrown into peril: the carriage could not wheel about in time, guards could not react in time—even Wuhuo's strength or Peng Meng's archery would be useless, and every dead trunk becomes a weapon against you.

sentences.zh: 是胡越起於轂下，而羌夷接軫也，豈不殆哉！

translations.literal: This is Hu and Yue rising under the wheel-hubs, and Qiang and Yi meeting the axle-trees—is it not perilous!
translations.idiomatic: It would be like barbarians springing up beneath the royal axle—could anything be more deadly?

sentences.zh: 雖萬全而無患，然本非天子之所宜近也。

translations.literal: Though utterly safe without calamity, yet fundamentally it is not what the Son of Heaven should draw near.
translations.idiomatic: Even if no disaster came, such sport is unworthy of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 且夫清道而後行，中路而馳，猶時有銜橛之變。

translations.literal: Moreover, when the road is cleared and then you proceed, galloping on the middle road, still sometimes there are bit-and-bridge accidents.
translations.idiomatic: Even when the avenue is cleared and you gallop down the highway, accidents of rein and bit still happen.

sentences.zh: 況乎涉豐草，騁丘虛，前有利獸之樂，而內無存變之意，其為害也不亦難矣！

translations.literal: How much more when you ford deep grass, gallop over barren hills, with the lure of fierce beasts ahead and no thought of lurking danger within—is not the harm then hard to foresee!
translations.idiomatic: How much worse to plunge through tall grass and race over open wastes, with the thrill of the kill ahead and no guard against ambush—the danger then is beyond reckoning!

sentences.zh: 夫輕萬乘之重不以為安，樂出萬有一危之塗以為娛，臣竊為陛下不取。

translations.literal: To slight the weight of the ten thousand chariots and not deem it security, to delight in going out on a path where danger exists once in ten thousand and take it as amusement—your servant privately holds that Your Majesty should not approve.
translations.idiomatic: To treat the safety of the imperial equipage lightly and seek pleasure on a road where ruin needs only one chance in ten thousand—your servant respectfully urges that Your Majesty should not choose this.

sentences.zh: 蓋明者遠見於未萌，而知者避危於無形，禍固多藏於隱微而發於人之所忽者也。

translations.literal: The enlightened see far before sprouting, the wise avoid danger before it takes form; calamity indeed often hides in the hidden and subtle and breaks out where men neglect it.
translations.idiomatic: The clear-sighted foresee trouble before it appears; the wise step aside before danger takes shape—most disasters nest in what is small and silent until they burst forth where no one was watching.

sentences.zh: 故鄙諺曰：「家累千金，坐不垂堂。」

translations.literal: Therefore a rustic proverb says: "When the household has piled up a thousand in gold, one does not sit beneath the overhanging eaves of the hall.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the proverb: a family rich enough to count its wealth in gold does not lounge under the exposed edge of a high roof.

sentences.zh: 此言雖小，可以諭大。

translations.literal: Though this saying is small, by it one may illustrate what is great.
translations.idiomatic: Small as the adage is, it speaks to a great truth.

sentences.zh: 臣願陛下留意幸察。

translations.literal: Your servant begs Your Majesty to take note and deign to examine.
translations.idiomatic: I beg Your Majesty to weigh these words with care.

sentences.zh: 上善之。

translations.literal: The emperor deemed it good.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved the memorial.

sentences.zh: 還過宜春宮，相如奏賦以哀二世行失。

translations.literal: On the return journey passing Yichun Palace, Xiangru submitted a fu to lament the faults in conduct of the Second Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: On the way back the imperial train passed Yichun Palace, where Xiangru offered a rhapsody mourning the Second Emperor's moral failure.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: The piece begins:

sentences.zh: 登陂□之長阪兮，坌入曾宮之嵯峨。

translations.literal: Ascending the long slope of the terraced bank ah, rushing into the layered palace's towering height.
translations.idiomatic: I climb the long terraced slope and sweep into palaces piled one above another, sheer as cliffs.

sentences.zh: 臨曲江之隑州兮，望南山之參差。

translations.literal: Overlooking the islet in the winding Qu River ah, gazing at the uneven southern mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Below me the river bends around its shoals; before me the southern peaks stagger in broken ranks.

sentences.zh: 巖巖深山之谾□兮，通谷豁乎谽。

translations.literal: Rock-steep, the deep mountain's hollow vastness ah, through valleys opening wide in yawning depth.
translations.idiomatic: The mountains lift savage crags; hollows open into gulfs where valleys yawn wide as a mouth.

sentences.zh: 汨淢靸以永逝兮，注平皋之廣衍。

translations.literal: Rushing, seething, gliding, forever flowing ah, pouring into the level marsh's broad expanse.
translations.idiomatic: The flood races on without rest until it spills across the flat marsh, wide as a sea of reeds.

sentences.zh: 觀眾樹之蓊薆兮，覽竹林之榛榛。

translations.literal: Observing the many trees' luxuriant density ah, viewing the bamboo grove thick and rank.
translations.idiomatic: Every tree stands in a tangle of shade; the bamboo runs on in an unbroken wall of green.

sentences.zh: 東馳土山兮，北揭石瀨。

translations.literal: Eastward galloping the earthen hill ah, northward fording the stony shallows.
translations.idiomatic: I wheel east past earthen knolls and strike north through stony rapids.

sentences.zh: 弭節容與兮，歷吊二世。

translations.literal: Checking the pace, lingering in ease ah, passing to lament the Second Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: I check the horses, let the carriage drift, and halt where the Second Emperor lies.

sentences.zh: 持身不謹兮，亡國失勢；

translations.literal: Not careful in holding the person ah, lost the state and forfeited power;
translations.idiomatic: He could not govern himself—his empire slipped away and his power failed;

sentences.zh: 信讒不寤兮，宗廟滅絕。

translations.literal: trusted slander without awakening ah, ancestral temples extinguished.
translations.idiomatic: he trusted slander to the end and never woke—till his ancestral shrines were cold ash.

sentences.zh: 烏乎！

translations.literal: Alas!
translations.idiomatic: Alas!

sentences.zh: 操行之不得，墓蕪穢而不修兮，魂亡歸而不食。

translations.literal: Conduct without attainment ah, tomb overgrown and untended; soul without returning home, unfed.
translations.idiomatic: His life came to nothing; his mound chokes with weeds and no hand tends it; his ghost wanders hungry, with no altar to feed it.

sentences.zh: 相如拜為孝文園令。

translations.literal: Xiangru was appointed director of the park of Emperor Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru received appointment as steward of Emperor Wen's mausoleum park.

sentences.zh: 上既美子虛之事，相如見上好仙，因曰：「上林之事未足美也，尚有靡者。

translations.literal: The emperor already admired the affair of Sir Fantasy; Xiangru saw the emperor loved immortals, and therefore said: "The affair of the Upper Forest is not enough to be admired; there is yet something more splendid.
translations.idiomatic: The sovereign had already praised his "Sir Fantasy" rhapsody. Seeing how fond the emperor was of transcendents, Xiangru said: "What I wrote of the Upper Grove is not the limit of splendor; something grander remains.

sentences.zh: 臣嘗為《大人賦》，未就，請具而奏之。」

translations.literal: Your servant once composed a "Rhapsody for the Great Man," unfinished; I beg to complete it and present it.
translations.idiomatic: I have drafted a "Rhapsody for the Great Man" but never finished it; allow me to polish it and lay it before you.

sentences.zh: 相如以為列仙之儒居山澤間，形容甚□，此非帝王之仙意也，乃遂奏《大人賦》。

translations.literal: Xiangru considered that the sort who rank as immortals dwell among mountains and marshes with forms very [wretched]; this was not the immortal intent proper to an emperor; thereupon he presented the "Rhapsody for the Great Man.
translations.idiomatic: He thought the common picture of immortals—emaciated recluses in hills and swamps—beneath an emperor's dignity, and so he offered the "Rhapsody for the Great Man" instead.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: It opens:

sentences.zh: 世有大人兮，在乎中州。

translations.literal: In the world there is a Great Man ah, dwelling in the central region.
translations.idiomatic: There walks among us a Great Man, and his seat is the Middle Kingdom.

sentences.zh: 宅彌萬里兮，曾不足以少留。

translations.literal: His domain fills ten thousand li ah, yet it is not enough to detain him briefly.
translations.idiomatic: His house is ten thousand leagues wide, yet he cannot bear to linger there an hour.

sentences.zh: 悲世俗之迫隘兮，朅輕舉而遠遊。

translations.literal: He grieves the vulgar world's cramped narrowness ah, and rises lightly to wander far.
translations.idiomatic: The world's meanness galls him; he shakes free and soars on a journey without end.

sentences.zh: 乘絳幡之素蜺兮，載雲氣而上浮。

translations.literal: Riding the crimson banner's white rainbow ah, bearing cloud-vapor and floating upward.
translations.idiomatic: He mounts a rainbow threaded through a scarlet banner and climbs on a tide of cloud.

sentences.zh: 建格澤之修竿兮，總光耀之采旄。

translations.literal: He raises the Geze star's tall pole ah, gathers the radiance of light into plumed streamers.
translations.idiomatic: He plants a pole tall as the Geze comet and braids sunbeams into streaming pennants.

sentences.zh: 垂旬始以為幓兮，曳慧星而為□。

translations.literal: He lets fall Xunshi as a curtain ah, trails a comet to serve as [banner].
translations.idiomatic: The comet Xunshi hangs like a canopy; a sweeping comet trails behind as his standard.

sentences.zh: 掉指橋以偃□兮，又猗抳以招搖。

translations.literal: He flicks the finger-bridge and lets it lie low ah, again leans and sways in rocking motion.
translations.idiomatic: His chariot poles flex and dip; the traces sway and swing as if beckoning the wind.

sentences.zh: 攬攙搶以為旌兮，靡屈虹而為綢。

translations.literal: He gathers chanqiang for a banner ah, spreads the bent rainbow for silken cords.
translations.idiomatic: He twists meteors into his ensigns and stretches the curving rainbow for reins.

sentences.zh: 紅杳眇以玄□兮，□風湧而雲浮。

translations.literal: Red, dim and vast in dark [depth] ah, [wind] surges and clouds float.
translations.idiomatic: A flush of red fades into purple depths; gusts boil up and clouds stream beneath him.

sentences.zh: 駕應龍象輿之蠖略委麗兮，驂赤螭青虯之蚴□宛蜓。

translations.literal: Driving the responding dragon's chariot, coiling and winding ah, with red hornless dragon and green horned dragon as outriders, writhing and twisting.
translations.idiomatic: His car is drawn by the winged yinglong, sinuous and long; scarlet chi and green qiu pace as outriders, bodies coiled like ropes.

sentences.zh: 低卬夭蟜裾以驕驁兮，詘折隆窮□以連捲。

translations.literal: Low and high, arching skirts in pride ah, bending and rising, heaped folds, linked in coils.
translations.idiomatic: The team arches and dips, manes flaring like proud banners, shoulders rolling in deep folds that link one curve to the next.

sentences.zh: 沛艾赳螑仡以佁儗兮，放散畔岸驤以孱顏。

translations.literal: Surging, rearing, towering in hesitation ah, scattering along the bank, tossing heads, sidelong glances.
translations.idiomatic: They surge and rear, hesitate and hold, then break along the shore, heads tossing, eyes rolling white.

sentences.zh: □踱輵螛容以骫麗兮，蜩□偃□怵彘以梁倚。

translations.literal: [Obscure graph] pacing, [obscure] bearing, bent and lovely ah, cicada[-like], leaning, trembling, leaning on the beam.
translations.idiomatic: Their gait is a slow, sliding dance—now crouched, now stretched—like creatures clinging to a rafter in fear.

sentences.zh: 糾蓼叫踏以□路兮，□蒙踴躍騰而狂□。

translations.literal: Twisting, calling, stamping on the [road] ah, [obscure] leaping and vaulting in wild bound.
translations.idiomatic: They twist, cry, stamp the way, then bound and leap in a frenzy of motion.

sentences.zh: 蒞颯□歙焱至電過兮，煥然霧除，霍然雲消。

translations.literal: Whirling, hissing, blazing, lightning-flash ah, suddenly mist clears, suddenly clouds vanish.
translations.idiomatic: They rush like wind, flash like lightning; in an instant mist lifts and clouds shred away.

sentences.zh: 邪絕少陽而登太陰兮，與真人乎相求。

translations.literal: Slanting past Shaoyang and mounting Taiyin ah, he seeks the company of the Perfected.
translations.idiomatic: He cuts across the lesser yang and climbs the greater yin, seeking the companionship of transcendents.

sentences.zh: 互折窈窕以右轉兮，橫厲飛泉以正東。

translations.literal: Crossing in hidden grace he turns to the right ah, spanning the flying spring straight east.
translations.idiomatic: He veers through winding depths toward the east, vaults the flying cataract, and holds a true eastward course.

sentences.zh: 悉征靈圉而選之兮，部署眾神於搖光。

translations.literal: He fully summons the numinous guards and selects them ah, arrays the host of spirits at Yaoguang.
translations.idiomatic: He calls every spirit warden, chooses his escort, and ranks the gods beneath the star Yaoguang.

sentences.zh: 使五帝先導兮，反大壹而從陵陽。

translations.literal: He makes the Five Thearchs go before as guides ah, turns back Grand Unity and follows Lingyang.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Thearchs lead the way; Taiyi falls in behind; Lingyang brings up the rear.

sentences.zh: 左玄冥而右黔雷兮，前長離而後矞皇。

translations.literal: Dark Void to the left, Black Thunder to the right ah, Long Li before, Yuhuang behind.
translations.idiomatic: Xuanming rides his left flank, Qianlei his right; Changli leads, Yuhuang follows.

sentences.zh: 廝征伯僑而役羨門兮，詔岐伯使尚方。

translations.literal: He presses Boyang into service and employs Xianmen ah, instructs Qibo to attend the imperial pharmacy.
translations.idiomatic: He drafts Boyang and Xianmen as runners and bids Qibo to ready the sovereign's medicaments.

sentences.zh: 祝融警而蹕御兮，清氣氛而後行。

translations.literal: Zhurong keeps watch and clears the road ah, purges foul vapor and then proceeds.
translations.idiomatic: Zhurong sounds the escort and clears the path; ill vapors scatter before he moves on.

sentences.zh: 屯余車而萬乘兮，綷雲蓋而樹華旗。

translations.literal: He halts his chariots, ten thousand teams ah, rainbow canopy piled, flowering banners planted.
translations.idiomatic: He parks ten thousand cars beneath a canopy of stacked rainbows and a forest of jeweled flags.

sentences.zh: 使句芒其將行兮，吾欲往乎南娭。

translations.literal: He orders Gou Mang to lead the march ah, I wish to go to the southern play.
translations.idiomatic: He sets Gou Mang at the head of the column, for I mean to visit the southern pleasure-grounds.

sentences.zh: 歷唐堯於崇山兮，過虞舜於九疑。

translations.literal: Passing Tang Yao at Mount Chong ah, passing Yu Shun at Jiuyi.
translations.idiomatic: He salutes Yao at Mount Chong and Shun among the peaks of Jiuyi.

sentences.zh: 紛湛湛差差錯兮，雜□膠輵以方馳。

translations.literal: Thickly, deeply, staggered and uneven ah, mixed [obscure], side by side, galloping abreast.
translations.idiomatic: His train piles deep—ranks staggered, teams interlaced—yet every car keeps pace abreast.

sentences.zh: 騷擾沖蓯其紛拏兮，滂濞泱軋麗以林離。

translations.literal: Restless, rushing, their tangled grasp ah, surging, rolling, beautiful in tangled separation.
translations.idiomatic: They boil forward in a tangle of hooves and wheels, a surging mass that breaks and re-forms like surf.

sentences.zh: 攢羅列聚叢以籠茸兮，衍曼流爛□以陸離。

translations.literal: Crowding, arraying, clustering thick ah, spreading, flowing, brilliant, [obscure] in shimmering scatter.
translations.idiomatic: They crowd like stacked sheaves, then spill in a long glittering flood, banners and chariots a shifting dazzle.

sentences.zh: 徑入雷室之砰磷鬱律兮，洞出鬼谷之堀□崴魁。

translations.literal: Straight into the Thunder Chamber's booming rumble ah, piercing out through Ghost Valley's jagged, towering gloom.
translations.idiomatic: He plunges into the Thunder God's vault where drums never cease, then bursts through Ghost Valley's broken teeth of rock.

sentences.zh: 遍覽八□而觀四海兮，朅度九江越五河。

translations.literal: He surveys the eight [regions] and views the four seas ah, crosses the nine rivers and spans the five streams.
translations.idiomatic: He scans the eight limits, eyes all four seas, crosses the nine streams, and rides the five great rivers.

sentences.zh: 經營炎火而浮弱水兮，杭絕浮渚涉流沙。

translations.literal: He passes through scorching fire and floats the weak water ah, ferries the floating islets and wades the shifting sands.
translations.idiomatic: He sails through infernal flame, drifts the Weak Water, poles past floating shoals, and wades the moving dunes.

sentences.zh: 奄息蔥極氾濫水娭兮，使靈媧鼓琴而舞馮夷。

translations.literal: Briefly resting on the onion-green summit, flooding wide in the water's play ah, he makes Lingwa strum the zither and Fengyi dance.
translations.idiomatic: He rests on the emerald peak, sports in the spreading flood, and sets Lingwa plucking strings while Fengyi dances for him.

sentences.zh: 時若曖曖將混濁兮，召屏翳誅風伯，刑雨師。

translations.literal: When the hour grows dim and thick with mingled murk ah, he summons Pingyi, punishes the Wind Earl, executes the Rain Master.
translations.idiomatic: When gloom closes and the air turns foul, he calls Pingyi, binds the Earl of Wind, and puts the Lord of Rain to the sword.

sentences.zh: 西望崑崙之軋沕荒忽兮，直徑馳乎三危。

translations.literal: Westward gazing at Kunlun's vast blur and haze ah, he drives straight to the three perils.
translations.idiomatic: He looks west where Kunlun blurs into mist, then gallops straight toward the Three Perils.

sentences.zh: 排閶闔而入帝宮兮，載玉女而與之歸。

translations.literal: He pushes open the Gate of Heaven and enters the emperor's palace ah, loads the jade maidens and returns with them.
translations.idiomatic: He thrusts aside the celestial gate, enters the High God's hall, and bears away the jade maidens in his train.

sentences.zh: 登閬風而遙集兮，亢鳥騰而壹止。

translations.literal: Ascending Langfeng, aloft in distant gathering ah, the dark birds soar and settle as one.
translations.idiomatic: He mounts Langfeng's height and hovers far aloft; black birds wheel up and settle in a single flock.

sentences.zh: 低徊陰山翔以紆曲兮，吾乃今日睹西王母。

translations.literal: Wheeling low by Yin Mountain, soaring in winding curves ah, only today do I behold the Queen Mother of the West.
translations.idiomatic: He circles Yin Mountain in slow spirals—at last I look upon the Queen Mother of the West.

sentences.zh: 暠然白首戴勝而穴處兮，亦幸有三足烏為之使。

translations.literal: Bright white of hair, she wears the sheng and dwells in a cave ah, yet is fortunate to have the three-legged crow as her messenger.
translations.idiomatic: Her hair gleams white; she wears the jade crown and lives in a stone cavern, yet commands the three-legged crow as herald.

sentences.zh: 必長生若此而不死兮，雖濟萬世不足以喜。

translations.literal: If long life must be thus and never die ah, though it lasted ten thousand ages it would not be enough for joy.
translations.idiomatic: If immortality meant only this—never dying, yet living thus—it would run ten thousand generations without bringing delight.

sentences.zh: 回車朅來兮，絕道不周，會食幽鬱。

translations.literal: Turning the chariot, he comes back ah, cuts the road at Mount Buzhou, feasts in somber gloom.
translations.idiomatic: He wheels his car and returns, breaks the road at Mount Buzhou, and takes his meal in shadowed stillness.

sentences.zh: 呼吸沆瀣兮餐朝霞，咀□芝英兮嘰瓊華。

translations.literal: Breathing the night vapor, dining on morning cloud ah, chewing [obscure] mushroom essence, tasting jade brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: He drinks the night mist, eats the dawn's rose cloud, chews the essence of magic fungi, and tastes petals of jade.

sentences.zh: 僸祲尋而高縱兮，紛鴻溶而上厲。

translations.literal: Soaring, darting, seeking height ah, in a welter of vast billows he mounts aloft.
translations.idiomatic: He shoots upward through swirling radiance, climbing on a swell of light.

sentences.zh: 貫列缺之倒景兮，涉豐隆之滂濞。

translations.literal: He pierces the inverted gleam of the lightning-fissure ah, crosses Fenglong's pouring flood.
translations.idiomatic: He threads the lightning's backward flash and rides the cataract where the thunder god walks.

sentences.zh: 騁游道而修降兮，騖遺霧而遠逝。

translations.literal: Galloping the wandering way, long descending ah, spurring through trailing mist, speeding far away.
translations.idiomatic: He races the roads of heaven on a long descent, drives through trailing mist, and vanishes into distance.

sentences.zh: 迫區中之隘陝兮，舒節出乎北垠。

translations.literal: Pressed by the narrow straits of the inner realm ah, he slackens the pace and issues beyond the northern bound.
translations.idiomatic: Stifled by the world's narrow lanes, he opens the reins and bursts out past the northern rim.

sentences.zh: 遺屯騎於玄闕兮，軼先驅於寒門。

translations.literal: Leaving the encamped riders at the Dark Tower ah, outstripping the vanguard at the Gate of Cold.
translations.idiomatic: He leaves his escort at the Dark Tower and outruns the vanguard at the Gate of Cold.

sentences.zh: 下崢嶸而無地兮，上□廓而無天。

translations.literal: Below, sheer depth without earth ah, above, [vast] emptiness without sky.
translations.idiomatic: Beneath him gulf on gulf with no ground; above, hollow without bound, with no sky to mark it.

sentences.zh: 視眩泯而亡見兮，聽敞怳而亡聞。

translations.literal: Vision dazzles, blurred, and sees nothing ah; hearing opens wide, vacant, and hears nothing.
translations.idiomatic: His eyes swim in blur until sight fails; his ears ring hollow until sound dies.

sentences.zh: 乘虛亡而上遐兮，超無友而獨存。

translations.literal: Riding void emptiness upward to the far distance ah, transcending without peers, alone surviving.
translations.idiomatic: He rides the empty dark beyond the highest reach, peerless, alone, the only thing that endures.

sentences.zh: 相如既奏《大人賦》，天子大說，飄飄有陵雲氣游天地之間意。

translations.literal: When Xiangru had presented the "Rhapsody for the Great Man," the Son of Heaven was greatly pleased, buoyantly as if he had the intent of cloud-riding spirit roaming between heaven and earth.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiangru recited the "Rhapsody for the Great Man," the emperor was transported; he felt light enough to ride the wind between heaven and earth.

sentences.zh: 相如既病免，家居茂陵。

translations.literal: When Xiangru had been dismissed on illness, he dwelt at home in Maoling.
translations.idiomatic: When illness forced Xiangru to retire, he lived quietly at Maoling.

sentences.zh: 天子曰：「司馬相如病甚，可往從悉取其書，若後之矣。」

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven said: "Sima Xiangru is gravely ill; someone may go and take all his writings from him, lest they come too late.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "Sima Xiangru is dying; send someone to collect whatever manuscripts he has before it is too late.

sentences.zh: 使所忠往，而相如已死，家無遺書。

translations.literal: He sent Suo Zhong; but Xiangru had already died, and his household had no writings left.
translations.idiomatic: Suo Zhong was dispatched, but Xiangru had already died and his house held no books.

sentences.zh: 問其妻，對曰：「長卿未嘗有書也。

translations.literal: Questioning his wife, she replied: "Changqing never had books.
translations.idiomatic: When they asked his wife, she said: "Changqing never kept books of his own.

sentences.zh: 時時著書，人又取去。

translations.literal: From time to time he wrote books, and people again took them away.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he wrote something, someone carried the draft away.

sentences.zh: 長卿未死時，為一卷書，曰有使來求書，奏之。」

translations.literal: Before Changqing died, he made one scroll of writing, saying, "If an envoy comes seeking writings, present this memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Before he died he sealed a single scroll with the words: if an envoy comes for my writings, submit this memorial to the throne.

sentences.zh: 其遺札書言封禪事，所忠奏焉，天子異之。

translations.literal: The farewell note he left discussed the feng and shan sacrifices; Suo Zhong presented it, and the Son of Heaven marveled at it.
translations.idiomatic: That final manuscript discussed the feng and shan sacrifices. Suo Zhong laid it before the throne, and the emperor received it with wonder.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: It reads:

sentences.zh: 伊上古之初肇，自顥穹生民。

translations.literal: Ah, in remotest antiquity at the first beginning, from the bright vault the people were born.
translations.idiomatic: In the dawn of time, when the bright sky first quickened the human race—

sentences.zh: 歷選列辟，以迄乎秦。

translations.literal: Through successive choice of many lords, down to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: —sovereigns rose in turn, chosen one after another, down to the house of Qin.

sentences.zh: 率邇者踵武，聽逖者風聲。

translations.literal: Those near trod in footprints; those far listened to report of conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Neighbors watched their predecessors' footprints; distant lands heard only their fame.

sentences.zh: 紛輪威蕤，堙滅而不稱者，不可勝數也。

translations.literal: In tangled glory, buried and extinguished without praise, they cannot be counted.
translations.idiomatic: Countless kings shone for a moment, then sank into silence without a name left to us.

sentences.zh: 繼《昭》、《夏》，崇號謚，略可道者七十有二君。

translations.literal: Continuing after "Zhao" and "Xia," exalting titles and posthumous names, those of whom something may be told number seventy-two lords.
translations.idiomatic: After the hymns of Shao and Xia, when styles and temple names were fixed, seventy-two rulers still left a trace in the record.

sentences.zh: 罔若淑而不昌，疇逆失而能存？

translations.literal: None who were compliant and good failed to flourish; who ever survived by turning contrary and losing the way?
translations.idiomatic: None who kept to the good ever failed to thrive; none who flouted the way long survived.

sentences.zh: 軒轅之前，遐哉邈乎，其詳不可得聞已。

translations.literal: Before the Yellow Thearch, how far, how remote—its details cannot be heard in full.
translations.idiomatic: Before the Yellow Thearch the past is too dim; no trustworthy detail survives.

sentences.zh: 五三《六經》載籍之傳，維見可觀也。

translations.literal: The Five, the Three, and the Six Classics' transmitted records—only what may be viewed remains.
translations.idiomatic: What the Five Thearchs, Three Ages, and Six Classics preserve is all we have to go on.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「元首明哉！

translations.literal: The Documents says: "The head is enlightened!
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Documents says: "Let the sovereign be clear-sighted!

sentences.zh: 股肱良哉！」

translations.literal: The thighs and arms are good!
translations.idiomatic: Let his ministers be worthy!

sentences.zh: 因斯以談，君莫盛於堯，臣莫賢於後稷。

translations.literal: From this to speak, no lord was greater than Yao, no minister worthier than Houji.
translations.idiomatic: Judging by that text, no ruler surpassed Yao and no minister equaled Houji.

sentences.zh: 後稷創業於唐，公劉發跡於西戎，文王改制，爰周郅隆，大行越成，而後陵遲衰微，千載亡聲，豈不善始善終哉！

translations.literal: Houji founded the enterprise at Tang; Gong Liu rose from the western Rong; King Wen reformed the institutions, and Zhou then reached its utmost height; great conduct crossed into completion, afterward declining and waning, a thousand years without renown—is this not a good beginning and good end!
translations.idiomatic: Houji began the work under Tang, Gong Liu raised the clan among the western Rong, King Wen reshaped the rites, and Zhou climbed to its zenith; their grand achievement was fulfilled, then slowly waned until a thousand years of silence followed—yet that is still what we call beginning well and ending well!

sentences.zh: 然無異端，慎所由於前，謹遺教於後耳。

translations.literal: Yet there were no aberrant paths; they were careful in the sources they took from the past, strict in the teachings they left to posterity.
translations.idiomatic: They never strayed into excess: they chose their models with care and handed down sober lessons to those who came after.

sentences.zh: 故軌跡夷易，易遵也；

translations.literal: Therefore their tracks were level and easy, easy to follow;
translations.idiomatic: Their footprints lie on level ground—easy for any heir to follow;

sentences.zh: 湛恩龐洪，易豐也；

translations.literal: their deep favor was vast and great, easy to enrich;
translations.idiomatic: their kindness ran deep and wide—easy for any heir to share;

sentences.zh: 憲度著明，易則也；

translations.literal: their statutes and measures were clear and bright, easy to take as model;
translations.idiomatic: their laws shone plain for all to see—easy to take as pattern;

sentences.zh: 垂統理順，易繼也。

translations.literal: the line they handed down was ordered and smooth, easy to continue.
translations.idiomatic: the succession they left was orderly—easy to continue without break.

sentences.zh: 是以業隆於繈保而崇冠乎二後。

translations.literal: Therefore their enterprise rose from the swaddling-cloth and crowned the two later [founders].
translations.idiomatic: So the dynasty's greatness grew from the cradle and reached its crown in the two later sage-kings.

sentences.zh: 揆厥所元，終都攸卒，未有殊尤絕跡可考於今者也。

translations.literal: Tracing back to its origin, to the final end where all gathers, there has been no matchless, supreme trace that can be examined in the present age.
translations.idiomatic: Yet from root to branch no age has left a deed so supreme that posterity can still measure itself against it.

sentences.zh: 然猶躡梁甫，登太山，建顯號，施尊名。

translations.literal: Yet still they trod Liangfu, ascended Mount Tai, established illustrious titles, applied exalted names.
translations.idiomatic: Even so they climbed Liangfu, ascended Tai Shan, proclaimed glorious titles, and assumed the noblest styles.

sentences.zh: 大漢之德，逢湧原泉，沕譎曼羨，旁魄四塞，雲布霧散，上暢九垓，下溯八埏。

translations.literal: The virtue of Great Han, meeting gushing spring source, vast and overflowing, spreading to the four borders, cloud-like deployed and mist-like scattered, flowing upward through the nine heavens, flowing downward through the eight extremities.
translations.idiomatic: The virtue of the great Han wells up like a spring, spreads without limit, floods the four quarters, rolls out like clouds and mist, rises through the nine heavens above, and washes the eight outer reaches below.

sentences.zh: 懷生之類，沾濡浸潤，協氣橫流，武節焱逝，爾□游原，迥闊泳末，首惡郁沒，□昧昭晰，昆蟲闓怪，回首面內。

translations.literal: Classes of living things, soaked and drenched, harmonious vapor flows across, martial rhythm flames and passes, you [obscure] roam the plain, far and wide swim the ends, chief evils dense and gone, [obscure] dimness bright and clear, insects and oddities, turn their heads and face inward.
translations.idiomatic: Every creature drinks it in; a gentle qi rolls everywhere; martial order flashes past; the worst offenders sink from sight and blind corners come into the light; even strange beasts and crawling things turn homeward toward the throne.

sentences.zh: 然後囿騶虞之珍群，徼麋鹿之怪獸，導一莖六穗於包，犧雙觡共抵之獸，獲周餘放龜於岐，招翠黃乘龍於沼。

translations.literal: Then in the park are herds of precious zouyu, cordoned strange beasts of elk and deer, guiding one stalk six ears in the wrapping, sacrificial beasts with double horns joined at the base, capturing the Zhou-surplus released tortoise at Qi, summoning the kingfisher-yellow dragon-riding at the marsh.
translations.idiomatic: The royal preserves now hold herds of zouyu, rare elk and deer penned for the altar, grain ears of six spikes on a single stalk wrapped as tribute, sacrificial oxen whose paired horns grow from one boss, the tortoise freed at Qi when Zhou fell, and the yellow dragon of omen summoned from the marsh.

sentences.zh: 鬼神接靈圉，賓於閒館。

translations.literal: Ghosts and spirits received the numinous guards as guests in the quiet lodges.
translations.idiomatic: Spirits welcome spirit wardens as guests in the silent halls.

sentences.zh: 奇物譎詭，俶倘窮變。

translations.literal: Strange things, devious and uncanny, beginning vast, exhausted in change.
translations.idiomatic: Portents multiply, each odder than the last, beginning in strangeness and ending in transformation without end.

sentences.zh: 欽哉，符瑞臻茲，猶以為薄，不敢道封禪。

translations.literal: Admirable! When tallies and omens have reached this point, still thinking it thin, not daring to speak of feng and shan.
translations.idiomatic: Such omens crowd in, yet the court still calls its merit slight and hesitates even to name the feng and shan sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 蓋周躍魚隕杭，休之以燎。

translations.literal: In cover Zhou leaped fish, fell grain vessel, rested them with burning.
translations.idiomatic: The house of Zhou received its sign when fish leaped into the king's boat and grain filled the vessel—then offered them up with fire.

sentences.zh: 微夫斯之為符也，以登介丘，不亦恧乎！

translations.literal: If not for these being omens, to ascend the small hill—is it not shameful!
translations.idiomatic: Without such signs to climb a mere knoll would have been shame enough.

sentences.zh: 進攘之道，何其爽與？

translations.literal: The way of advance and yielding—how could it be so wrong!
translations.idiomatic: How twisted is a course that grasps at glory yet shrinks from the rite that crowns it!

sentences.zh: 於是大司馬進曰：「陛下仁育群生，義征不譓，諸夏樂貢，百蠻執贄，德牟往初，功無與二，休烈液洽，符瑞眾變，斯應紹至，不特創見。

translations.literal: Thereupon the grand marshal of state advanced and said: "Your Majesty humanely nurtures the myriad creatures, righteously punishes the disobedient; the various Xia delight in tribute, the hundred Man grasp their gifts; virtue exceeds the beginnings of old, achievement has no second; splendid merit spreads in fusion; tallies and omens crowd in change; such responses come in succession, not merely first creation.
translations.idiomatic: The commander-in-chief then stepped forward: "Your Majesty nurtures all life with humanity and chastises the stubborn with justice. The Hua states gladly bring tribute; barbarian chiefs arrive with gifts. Your virtue outshines antiquity; your deeds have no peer. Glory spreads abroad; omens arrive in endless variety—this is no first-time wonder but heaven's steady answer.

sentences.zh: 意者太山、梁父設壇場望幸，蓋號以況榮，上帝垂恩儲祉，，將以慶成，陛下嗛讓而弗發也。

translations.literal: I suppose Mount Tai and Liangfu have set altar platforms hoping for your fortunate visit, intending titles to match the glory; High God hangs down favor and stores blessing, intending thereby to celebrate completion; Your Majesty humbly yields and does not initiate.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Tai and Mount Liangfu have long stood ready with their altars, waiting for your chariot so their names may share your glory. High God heaps favor upon you to mark the work fulfilled—yet you modestly hold back and will not begin.

sentences.zh: 挈三神之歡，缺王道之儀，群臣恧焉。

translations.literal: To withhold the joy of the three spirits, to lack the rites of the kingly way—the various ministers are ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: To deny the Three Powers their joy and leave the kingly sacrifice incomplete shames every minister at court.

sentences.zh: 或謂且天為質□，示珍符固不可辭；

translations.literal: Some say moreover heaven is pledge [obscure], showing precious tallies indeed cannot be refused;
translations.idiomatic: Some argue that heaven itself is bond, that the rare talismans it sends cannot decently be refused;

sentences.zh: 若然辭之，是泰山靡記而梁父罔幾也。

translations.literal: If so and you refuse them, then Mount Tai has no record and Liangfu has no expectation.
translations.idiomatic: and that to refuse them would leave Tai Shan without its inscription and Liangfu without its hope of rite.

sentences.zh: 亦各並時而榮，鹹濟厥世而屈，說者尚何稱於後，而雲七十二君哉？

translations.literal: Also each in its time flourished, all aided their age then bent—what would speakers still praise in later times, saying seventy-two lords?
translations.idiomatic: The seventy-two ancient kings each rose in his season, served his age, then bowed out—what would later ages praise in them if you break the chain now?

sentences.zh: 夫修德以錫符，奉符以行事，不為進越也。

translations.literal: For cultivating virtue to grant tallies, receiving tallies to conduct affairs—is not advancing beyond.
translations.idiomatic: To earn heaven's tallies by virtue and to act once they are granted is not presumption—it is the way of the sage.

sentences.zh: 故聖王弗替，而修禮地祇，謁款天神，勒功中岳，以章至尊，舒盛德，發號榮，受厚福，以浸黎民。

translations.literal: Therefore the sage kings did not abandon it, but cultivated ritual to the earth spirits, sincerely approached the heavenly spirits, inscribed merit on the central marchmount, thereby to manifest the supreme, unfold flourishing virtue, issue glorious titles, receive thick blessing, thereby to soak the black-haired people.
translations.idiomatic: That is why sage kings never set the rite aside: they perfected worship of earth, made humble approach to heaven, carved their achievement on the sacred peak, displayed the supremacy of the throne, unfolded deep virtue, proclaimed a noble style, drew down rich blessing, and let it flood the common people.

sentences.zh: 皇皇哉斯事，天下之壯觀，王者之卒業，不可貶也。

translations.literal: Bright bright is this affair—the realm's grand sight, the king's concluding work—it cannot be belittled.
translations.idiomatic: This is the supreme spectacle of the empire and the capstone of kingship; it must not be slighted.

sentences.zh: 願陛下全之。

translations.literal: I beg Your Majesty to complete it.
translations.idiomatic: We beg you to bring it to fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 而後因雜縉紳先生之略術，使獲曜日月之末光絕炎，以展采錯事。

translations.literal: Afterward thereby mixing the rough arts of girdle-and-ribbon gentlemen, causing them to obtain to glisten in the last rays and dying gleam of sun and moon, thereby to unfold their colors and arrange affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Then let your scholars catch a reflected gleam of your glory and set their talents to the task.

sentences.zh: 猶兼正列其義，祓飾厥文，作《春秋》一藝。

translations.literal: Still together correcting and arraying their meanings, cleansing and adorning their texts, making the single art of the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Let them align the doctrine, polish the prose, and add a chapter worthy of the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 將襲舊六為七，攄之無窮，俾萬世得激清流，揚微波，蜚英聲，騰茂實。

translations.literal: Will inherit the old six as seven, spreading them without end, causing ten thousand generations to be stirred by clear current, raise slight wave, fly splendid sound, leap luxuriant substance.
translations.idiomatic: The canon will swell from six classics to seven, its influence endless, so that ages to come may ride its clear stream, catch its ripples, and carry its fame and solid achievement abroad.

sentences.zh: 前聖之所以永保鴻名而常為稱首者用此。

translations.literal: Former sages thereby forever preserved vast name and constantly were called chief—by using this.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier sages kept their great names forever at the head of the record by doing exactly this.

sentences.zh: 宜命掌故悉奏其儀而覽焉。」

translations.literal: It is fitting to order the archivist fully to memorialize their rites and inspect them.
translations.idiomatic: Order the archivists to set down the full ritual program for your review.

sentences.zh: 於是天子沛然改容，曰：「俞乎，朕其試哉！」

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven buoyantly changed countenance, saying: "Yes indeed, I will try it!
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's face cleared; he said, "Very well—I will make the attempt!

sentences.zh: 乃遷思回慮，總公卿之議，詢封禪之事，詩大澤之博，廣符瑞之富。

translations.literal: Thereupon shifting thought, turning consideration, gathering the deliberations of dukes and ministers, inquiring into feng and shan affairs, extolling the vastness of the great marsh, broadening the richness of tallies and omens.
translations.idiomatic: He gathered his wits, polled his high ministers on the feng and shan, praised the breadth of the great marsh, and rehearsed the wealth of recent omens.

sentences.zh: 遂作頌曰：

translations.literal: Thereupon he made a eulogy, saying:
translations.idiomatic: He then composed this hymn:

sentences.zh: 自我天覆，雲之油油。

translations.literal: From me heaven covers, clouds oily-oily.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven spreads its canopy above us; clouds roll in soft billows.

sentences.zh: 甘露時雨，厥壤可游。

translations.literal: Sweet dew, timely rain—its soil may be roamed.
translations.idiomatic: Sweet dew and seasonable rain make every field a pleasure to walk.

sentences.zh: 滋液滲漉，何生不育！

translations.literal: Moisture soaking and seeping—what life does it not nurture!
translations.idiomatic: Moisture seeps everywhere—what could fail to grow?

sentences.zh: 嘉谷六穗，我穡曷蓄？

translations.literal: Fine grain six ears—how should we store our harvest?
translations.idiomatic: The good grain bears six ears to a stalk—how can we hoard enough of such bounty?

sentences.zh: 匪唯雨之，又潤澤之；

translations.literal: Not only does it rain them, it also moistens them;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven sends not rain alone but a soaking kindness;

sentences.zh: 匪唯偏我，泛布護之；

translations.literal: not only does it favor me, it broadly spreads and shelters them;
translations.idiomatic: it favors not us only but spreads shelter over all;

sentences.zh: 萬物熙熙，懷而慕之。

translations.literal: The myriad things joyful, cherishing and longing for it.
translations.idiomatic: the myriad creatures thrive and turn toward it with love.

sentences.zh: 名山顯位，望君之來。

translations.literal: Famous mountains in manifest rank, looking for the lord's coming.
translations.idiomatic: The great peaks stand in ordered rank, watching for the sovereign's approach.

sentences.zh: 君兮君兮，侯不邁哉！

translations.literal: Lord ah lord ah, why do you not stride forward!
translations.idiomatic: O our king, why do you still hold back your step?

sentences.zh: □之獸，樂我君圃；

translations.literal: [Obscure] beast, delights in our lord's park;
translations.idiomatic: The zouyu takes joy in our ruler's park;

sentences.zh: 白質黑章，其儀可喜；

translations.literal: white substance black markings, its bearing is pleasing;
translations.idiomatic: white hide barred with black, a creature of lovely mien;

sentences.zh: 旼□穆穆，君子之態。

translations.literal: gentle [obscure], solemn and grave, the bearing of a gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: gentle, solemn, grave—the very air of a gentleman.

sentences.zh: 蓋聞其聲，今視其來。

translations.literal: I had heard its name, now I see its coming.
translations.idiomatic: We knew it only by report; now we watch it arrive.

sentences.zh: 厥塗靡從，天瑞之征。

translations.literal: Its track has none to follow—an omen of heaven's favor.
translations.idiomatic: No footprints mark its path—sure sign of heaven's grace.

sentences.zh: 慈爾於舜，虞氏以興。

translations.literal: You are like Shun; the Yu clan thereby rose.
translations.idiomatic: Such kindness shone in Shun, and the house of Yu rose on it.

sentences.zh: 濯濯之麟，游彼靈畤。

translations.literal: The glossy unicorn, roaming that spirit altar-field.
translations.idiomatic: The sleek qilin wanders the sacred enclosure of the spirit altar.

sentences.zh: 孟冬十月，君徂郊祀。

translations.literal: Tenth month of winter, the lord goes to suburban sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth winter month our lord drives out to the suburban offering.

sentences.zh: 馳我君輿，帝用享祉。

translations.literal: Galloping our lord's chariot, the Thearch thereby enjoys blessing.
translations.idiomatic: His chariot flies to the rite, and High God accepts the blessing.

sentences.zh: 三代之前，蓋未嘗有。

translations.literal: Before the three dynasties, in cover never had there been.
translations.idiomatic: In the three earlier dynasties such a thing had never been recorded.

sentences.zh: 宛宛黃龍，興德而升；

translations.literal: Winding winding yellow dragon, rising with virtue and ascending;
translations.idiomatic: The sinuous yellow dragon mounts on virtue and climbs the sky;

sentences.zh: 采色玄耀，炳炳輝煌。

translations.literal: colors darkly blazing, blazing brilliant.
translations.idiomatic: its colors blaze darkly, flash with dazzling fire.

sentences.zh: 正陽顯見，覺寤黎烝。

translations.literal: Correct yang manifestly appears, awakening the black-haired masses.
